xText:
Day 7/111: Studied React Server Components.
Built/practiced: A small RSC demo with async data fetching.
Takeaway: RSC and Client Components solve different problems — knowing which to reach for matters.
#111DaysOfLearningForChange #CodeForChange
liText:
Day 7 of 111 — React Server Components.

Today I dug into React Server Components and what actually separates them from classic Client Components. The mental model shift is real: RSC runs on the server, has zero JS bundle cost, and can fetch data directly. Client Components handle interactivity.

What I built: a small demo with an async RSC that fetches from a mock API and passes props to a client-side interactive widget below it.

The biggest takeaway: the boundary between server and client is architectural, not cosmetic. Choosing the wrong one is how apps get slow.

Showing up every day is the skill.

#111DaysOfLearningForChange #CodeForChange #LearningInPublic
